task_completion.visual_evidence: Rocket.Chat interface is visible and the user is logged in (e.g., admin workspace or profile is accessible).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Personal Access Tokens section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat 'My Account' or settings page is open, specifically showing the 'Personal Access Tokens' tab or menu item.

task_completion.description: Create a new Personal Access Token named 'gitlab-ci'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A token creation form or the active tokens list displays a token with the exact name 'gitlab-ci'.

task_completion.description: The generated token string is displayed
task_completion.visual_evidence: A modal or screen in Rocket.Chat displays the newly generated token string (a long alphanumeric string) which is only shown once upon creation.
A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) or terminal window shows the file '/home/ga/gitlab_token.txt' containing the generated token string.

integrity.description: Agent used the Rocket.Chat GUI to generate the token
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent interacting with the actual Rocket.Chat web interface to create the token, rather than generating a random string via terminal or script.

integrity.description: The output file contains only the token string
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor or terminal output for /home/ga/gitlab_token.txt shows only a single alphanumeric string with no extra text, explanations, or task description copy-pasted.

integrity.description: The saved token matches the generated token
integrity.visual_evidence: The token string saved in /home/ga/gitlab_token.txt visually matches the token string that was displayed in the Rocket.Chat token generation modal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The token must be named exactly 'gitlab-ci'. The output file must be exactly '/home/ga/gitlab_token.txt'. The generated token string must be at least 20 characters long.
